verse: For God had such love for the world that he gave his only Son, so that whoever has faith in him may not come to destruction but have eternal life.
Come to me, all you who are troubled and weighted down with care, and I will give you rest.
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you: not as the world gives, do I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled, and let it have no fear.
Have no fear, for I am with you; be not overcome with trouble, for I am your God; I will give you strength; yes, I will be your helper; yes, my true right hand will be your support.
I am able to do all things through him who gives me strength.
Have no cares; but in everything with prayer and praise put your requests before God.
And we are conscious that all things are working together for good to those who have love for God, and have been marked out by his purpose.
Put all your hope in God and have no trust in the wisdom of your own mind. In all your ways give ear to him, and he will make straight your footsteps.
God is our safe place and our strength, ever ready to help in times of trouble.
Have I not given you your orders? Be strong and take heart; have no fear and do not be troubled, for the Lord your God is with you wherever you go.
But those who are waiting for the Lord will have new strength; their wings will be like those of an eagle: running, they will not be tired, and walking, they will have no weariness.
The Lord is near the broken-hearted; he is the saviour of those whose spirits are crushed down.
Put your troubles on the Lord, and he will be your support; he will not let the upright man be moved.
For I am conscious of my thoughts about you, says the Lord, thoughts of peace and not of evil, to give you hope at the end.
For our life is guided by faith, not by sight.
And he said to me, My grace is enough for you, for my power is made complete in what is feeble.
Putting all your troubles on him, for he takes care of you.
Our love is because he first had love for us.

For I am certain that not death, or life, or angels, or principalities, or things present, or things to come, or powers, or height, or depth, or any other living thing, will be able to make us separate from the love of God which is ours in Christ Jesus our Lord.
And he will make all their grief come to an end; and there will be no more death or sorrow or crying or pain; for the first things have come to an end.
The man whose heart is unmoved you will keep in peace, because his hope is in you.
The Lord is my keeper; I will have need of nothing.
Yes, though I go through the valley of the shadow of death, I will have no fear of evil; for you are with me; your rod and your support give me comfort.
Put your way into the hands of the Lord; have faith in him and he will do it.
So take your delight in the Lord, and he will give you your heart's desires.
The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom will I go in fear of? The Lord is the strength of my life; before whom will I be without hope?
Give ear to my cry and I will give you an answer, and let you see great things and secret things of which you had no knowledge.
But if any man is in need of wisdom, let him make his request to God, who gives freely to all without bitter words, and it will be given to him.
Then let us come with a good heart to the throne of grace, that we may have mercy and get grace in time of need.
Be free from the love of money, resting satisfied with such things as you have; for he has said, I will never go from you or give you up.
Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, and the sign that the things not seen are true.
See what great love the Father has given to us, naming us children of God; and such we are.
I have said these things to you so that you may have peace in me. In the world you have trouble: but take heart! I have overcome the world.
Let not your heart be troubled: have faith in God and have faith in me.
I have come so that they may have life, and may have it in greater measure.
If God is for us, who is against us?
For this cause those who are in Christ Jesus will not be judged as sinners.
Keep on with your prayers.
Be glad in the Lord at all times: again I say, Be glad.
And the peace of God, which is deeper than all knowledge, will keep your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.
And truly, I will be with you, and will keep you wherever you go, guiding you back again to this land; and I will not give you up till I have done what I have said to you.
The Lord will make war for you, you have only to keep quiet.
Be strong and take heart, and have no fear of them: for it is the Lord your God who is going with you; he will not take away his help from you.
It is the Lord who goes before you; he will be with you, he will not take away his help from you or give you up: so have no fear.
I will take my rest on my bed in peace, because you only, Lord, keep me safe.
And such were some of you; but you have been washed, you have been made holy, you have been given righteousness in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
I have put the Lord before me at all times; because he is at my right hand, I will not be moved.
The Lord is my Rock, my walled town, and my saviour; my God, my Rock, in him will I put my faith; my breastplate, and the horn of my salvation, and my high tower.
Let the words of my mouth and the thoughts of my heart be pleasing in your eyes, O Lord, my strength and my salvation.
Let your hope be in the Lord: take heart and be strong; yes, let your hope be in the Lord.
Put away fear and let your heart be strong, all you whose hope is in the Lord.
I will give you knowledge, teaching you the way to go; my eye will be your guide.
I was searching for the Lord, and he gave ear to my voice, and made me free from all my fears.
Be at peace in the knowledge that I am God: I will be lifted up among the nations, I will be honoured through all the earth.
In the time of my fear, I will have faith in you.
My flesh and my heart are wasting away: but God is the Rock of my heart and my eternal heritage.
So that glory may be given to the name of our Lord Jesus through you, and you may have glory in him, by the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Among all my troubled thoughts, your comforts are the delight of my soul.
For the Lord is good, and his mercy is never-ending; his faith is unchanging through all generations.
This is the day which the Lord has made; we will be full of joy and delight in it.
Or things on high, or things under the earth, or anything which is made, will be able to come between us and the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Your help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth.
The Lord will make all things complete for me: O Lord, your mercy is eternal; do not give up the works of your hands.
Put your works into the hands of the Lord, and your purposes will be made certain.
The name of the Lord is a strong tower: the upright man running into it is safe.
See, God is my salvation; I will have faith in the Lord, without fear: for the Lord Jah is my strength and song; and he has become my salvation.
Let your hope be in the Lord for ever: for the Lord Jah is an unchanging Rock.
And at your back, when you are turning to the right hand or to the left, a voice will be sounding in your ears, saying, This is the way in which you are to go.
When you go through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they will not go over you: when you go through the fire, you will not be burned; and the flame will have no power over you.
See, I am doing a new thing; now it is starting; will you not take note of it? I will even make a way in the waste land, and rivers in the dry country.
For the mountains may be taken away, and the hills be moved out of their places, but my love will not be taken from you, or my agreement of peace broken, says the Lord, who has had mercy on you.
They are new every morning; great is your good faith.
But let your first care be for his kingdom and his righteousness; and all these other things will be given to you in addition.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. So be it.
And Jesus, looking at them, said, With men this is not possible; but with God all things are possible.
Jesus, looking on them, said, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for all things are possible with God.
For there is nothing which God is not able to do.
And this was the answer of Jesus: I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will never be in need of food, and he who has faith in me will never be in need of drink.
Then again Jesus said to them, I am the light of the world; he who comes with me will not be walking in the dark but will have the light of life.
I am the vine, you are the branches: he who is in me at all times as I am in him, gives much fruit, because without me you are able to do nothing.
Grace be with all those who have true love for our Lord Jesus Christ.
Now may the God of hope make you full of joy and peace through faith, so that all hope may be yours in the power of the Holy Spirit.

You have been put to no test but such as is common to man: and God is true, who will not let any test come on you which you are not able to undergo; but he will make with the test a way out of it, so that you may be able to go through it.
But now we still have faith, hope, love, these three; and the greatest of these is love.
For which cause we do not give way to weariness; but though our outer man is getting feebler, our inner man is made new day by day.
For by his act we were given existence in Christ Jesus to do those good works which God before made ready for us so that we might do them.
Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ Jesus, sends you his love, ever taking thought for you in his prayers, that you may be complete and fully certain of all the purpose of God.
Lastly, be strong in the Lord, and in the strength of his power.
And let the peace of Christ be ruling in your hearts, as it was the purpose of God for you to be one body; and give praise to God at all times.
For God did not give us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of self-control.
And he had faith in the Lord, and it was put to his account as righteousness.
The Lord is my strength and my strong helper, he has become my salvation: he is my God and I will give him praise; my father's God and I will give him glory.
Let the word of Christ be in you in all wealth of wisdom; teaching and helping one another with songs of praise and holy words, making melody to God with grace in your hearts.
O Lord, have mercy on us; for we have been waiting for your help: be our strength every morning, our salvation in time of trouble.
May the Lord's approval be resting on you and may he give you peace.

And if it seems evil to you to be the servants of the Lord, make the decision this day whose servants you will be: of the gods whose servants your fathers were across the River, or of the gods of the Amorites in whose land you are living: but I and my house will be the servants of the Lord.
No other is holy as the Lord, for there is no other God but you: there is no Rock like our God.
As for God, his way is all good: the word of the Lord is tested; he is a safe cover for all those who put their faith in him.
Let your search be for the Lord and for his strength; let your hearts ever be turned to him.
O give praise to the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy is unchanging for ever.

Then he said to them, Go away now, and take the fat for your food and the sweet for your drink, and send some to him for whom nothing is made ready: for this day is holy to our Lord: and let there be no grief in your hearts; for the joy of the Lord is your strong place.
For he has knowledge of the way I take; after I have been tested I will come out like gold.
He will be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, which gives its fruit at the right time, whose leaves will ever be green; and he will do well in all his undertakings.
My voice will come to you in the morning, O Lord; in the morning will I send my prayer to you, and keep watch.
You will make clear to me the way of life; where you are joy is complete; in your right hand there are pleasures for ever and ever.
Some put their faith in carriages and some in horses; but we will be strong in the name of the Lord our God.
Make your steps clear to me, O Lord; give me knowledge of your ways.
Be my guide and teacher in the true way; for you are the God of my salvation; I am waiting for your word all the day.
The Lord will give strength to his people; the Lord will give his people the blessing of peace.
For his wrath is only for a minute; in his grace there is life; weeping may be for a night, but joy comes in the morning.
Our souls are waiting for the Lord; he is our help and our salvation.
By experience you will see that the Lord is good; happy is the man who has faith in him.
Take your rest in the Lord, waiting quietly for him; do not be angry because of the man who does well in his evil ways, and gives effect to his bad designs.
But let us, who are of the day, be serious, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and on our heads, the hope of salvation.
Make a clean heart in me, O God; give me a right spirit again.
Truly, the hills, and the noise of an army on the mountains, are a false hope: truly, in the Lord our God is the salvation of Israel.
My soul, put all your faith in God; for from him comes my hope.
The Lord God is our sun and our strength: the Lord will give grace and glory: he will not keep back any good thing from those whose ways are upright.
You are good, O Lord, and full of forgiveness; your mercy is great to all who make their cry to you.
The Lord is kind and full of pity, not quickly made angry, but ever ready to have mercy.
As far as the east is from the west, so far has he put our sins from us.
Preaching to Jews and to Greeks the need for a turning of the heart to God, and faith in the Lord Jesus Christ.
The Lord will keep you safe from all evil; he will take care of your soul.
The Lord is near all those who give honour to his name; even to all who give honour to him with true hearts.
He makes the broken-hearted well, and puts oil on their wounds.
For what is our hope or joy or crown of glory? Are not even you, before our Lord Jesus, at his coming?
And keep watch over your heart with all care; so you will have life.
A glad heart makes a healthy body, but a crushed spirit makes the bones dry.
O Lord, you are my God; I will give praise to you, I will give honour to your name; for you have done great acts of power; your purposes in the past have been made true and certain in effect.
He gives power to the feeble, increasing the strength of him who has no force.
And that every tongue may give witness that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

And the Lord will be your guide at all times; in dry places he will give you water in full measure, and will make strong your bones; and you will be like a watered garden, and like an ever-flowing spring.
A blessing is on the man who puts his faith in the Lord, and whose hope the Lord is.

For he will be like a tree planted by the waters, pushing out its roots by the stream; he will have no fear when the heat comes, but his leaf will be green; in a dry year he will have no care, and will go on giving fruit.
He has made clear to you, O man, what is good; and what is desired from you by the Lord; only doing what is right, and loving mercy, and walking without pride before your God.
The Lord is good, a strong place in the day of trouble; and he has knowledge of those who take him for their safe cover.
Still, I will be glad in the Lord, my joy will be in the God of my salvation.
Happy are the clean in heart: for they will see God.
Happy are the peacemakers: for they will be named sons of God.
Brothers, my heart's desire and my prayer to God for them is, that they may get salvation.
Be full of pity, even as your Father is full of pity.
So that with one mouth you may give glory to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
God did not send his Son into the world to be judge of the world; he sent him so that the world might have salvation through him.
If then the son makes you free, you will be truly free.
And they said, Have faith in the Lord Jesus, and you and your family will have salvation.
For you are my hope, O Lord God; I have had faith in you from the time when I was young.
For sin may not have rule over you: because you are not under law, but under grace.
Because, Whoever will give worship to the name of the Lord will get salvation.
Christ has truly made us free: then keep your free condition and let no man put a yoke on you again.
For the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, said, In quiet and rest is your salvation: peace and hope are your strength: but you would not have it so.
How beautiful on the mountains are the feet of him who comes with good news, who gives word of peace, saying that salvation is near; who says to Zion, Your God is ruling!
Keep your mind on the higher things, not on the things of earth.
There is for us only one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we are for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we have our being through him.
Every good and true thing is given to us from heaven, coming from the Father of lights, with whom there is no change or any shade made by turning.
Come near to God and he will come near to you. Make your hands clean, you evil-doers; put away deceit from your hearts, you false in mind.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. So be it.
He took our sins on himself, giving his body to be nailed on the tree, so that we, being dead to sin, might have a new life in righteousness, and by his wounds we have been made well.
If we say openly that we have done wrong, he is upright and true to his word, giving us forgiveness of sins and making us clean from all evil.
There is no fear in love: true love has no room for fear, because where fear is, there is pain; and he who is not free from fear is not complete in love.
Let this be my last word, brothers; be glad; be complete; be comforted; be of the same mind; be at peace with one another: and the God of love and peace will be with you.
Give praise to the Lord, you his angels, who are great in strength, doing his orders, and waiting for his voice.
If you send out your spirit, they are given life; you make new the face of the earth.

And in that day it will be said, See, this is our God; we have been waiting for him, and he will be our saviour: this is the Lord in whom is our hope; we will be glad and have delight in his salvation.
This is the word of the holy God: I will be glad; I will make Shechem a heritage, measuring out the valley of Succoth.
Let this be the reward given to my haters from the Lord, and to those who say evil of my soul.
He will have no fear of evil news; his heart is fixed, for his hope is in the Lord.
Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name let glory be given, because of your mercy and your unchanging faith.
Come back to your rest, O my soul; for the Lord has given you your reward.
The Lord is my strength and my song; he has become my salvation.
The sound of joy and salvation is in the tents of the upright; the right hand of the Lord does works of power.
It is better to have faith in the Lord than to put one's hope in man.
It is better to have faith in the Lord than to put one's hope in rulers.
Lord, my hope has been in your salvation; and I have kept your teachings.
But be increased in grace and in the knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. May he have glory now and for ever. So be it.
I am waiting for the Lord, my soul is waiting for him, and my hope is in his word.
O Israel, have hope in the Lord; for with the Lord is mercy and full salvation.
O Israel, have hope in the Lord, from this time and for ever.
May the salvation of Israel come out of Zion! when the fate of his people is changed by the Lord, Jacob will have joy and Israel will be glad.
O Lord God, the strength of my salvation, you have been a cover over my head in the day of the fight.
But my eyes are turned to you, O Lord God: my hope is in you; let not my soul be given up to death.
O Lord, take me out of the hands of my haters; my soul is waiting for you.
The Lord takes pleasure in his worshippers, and in those whose hope is in his mercy.

But keep yourself from those desires of the flesh which are strong when the body is young, and go after righteousness, faith, love, peace, with those whose prayers go up to the Lord from a clean heart.
Everyone who has faith that Jesus is the Christ is a child of God: and everyone who has love for the Father has love for his child.
Now am I certain that the Lord gives salvation to his king; he will give him an answer from his holy heaven with the strength of salvation in his right hand.

For this cause the Lord will be waiting, so that he may be kind to you; and he will be lifted up, so that he may have mercy on you; for the Lord is a God of righteousness: there is a blessing on all whose hope is in him.
For with the heart man has faith to get righteousness, and with the mouth he says that Jesus is Lord to get salvation.
I had almost given up my hope of seeing the blessing of the Lord in the land of the living.

And he said to me, O man greatly loved, have no fear: peace be with you, be strong and let your heart be lifted up. And at his words I became strong, and said, Let my lord say on, for you have given me strength.
The Lord is their strength, and a strong place of salvation for his king.
Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
But your strength, O Lord, is round me, you are my glory and the lifter up of my head.
But you, O Lord, are a God full of pity and forgiveness, slow to get angry, great in mercy and wisdom.
O have love for the Lord, all you his saints; for the Lord keeps safe from danger all those who are true to him, and gives the workers of pride their right reward.
While their hearts go out to you in love and in prayer for you, because of the great grace of God which is in you.
I am full of hate for those who go after false gods; but my hope is in the Lord.
For the Lord's heart is on Zion, desiring it for his resting-place.
Come back, O Lord, to your resting-place; you and the ark of your strength.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, and the love of God, and the harmony of the Holy Spirit, be with you all.
May grace, mercy, and peace be with us from God the Father, and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in all true love.
O Lord, you have made my soul come again from the underworld: you have given me life and kept me from going down among the dead.
Lord, by your grace you have kept my mountain strong: when your face was turned from me I was troubled.
How good is your loving mercy, O God! the children of men take cover under the shade of your wings.
But to me your word is a joy, making my heart glad; for I am named by your name, O Lord God of armies.
Take not away your gentle mercies from me, O Lord; let your mercy and your faith keep me safe for ever.
The Lord will keep him safe, and give him life; the Lord will let him be a blessing on the earth, and will not give him into the hand of his haters.
Because you have taken my soul from the power of death; and kept my feet from falling, so that I may be walking before God in the light of life.
May the seat of his authority be before God for ever; may mercy and righteousness keep him safe.
Give words of love to every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me send you their love.
O God, let your eyes be on him who is our safe cover, and let your heart be turned to your king.
You will have a song, as in the night when a holy feast is kept; and you will be glad in heart, as when they go with music of the pipe to the mountain of the Lord, the Rock of Israel.
The fear of man is a cause of danger: but whoever puts his faith in the Lord will have a safe place on high.
The Lord has kept us in mind and will give us his blessing; he will send blessings on the house of Israel and on the house of Aaron.
The Lord keeps the simple; I was made low, and he was my saviour.
I have kept the memory of your decisions from times past, O Lord; and they have been my comfort.
I have given thought to your name in the night, O Lord, and have kept your law.
You will keep them, O Lord, you will keep them safe from this generation for ever.
The Lord is your keeper; the Lord is your shade on your right hand.
Till I have got a place for the Lord, a resting-place for the great God of Jacob.
O Lord, take me out of the hands of sinners; keep me safe from the violent man: for they are designing my downfall.
For I have kept the ways of the Lord; I have not been turned away in sin from my God.
I will send up my cry to the Lord, who is to be praised; so will I be made safe from those who are against me.
He says in his heart, God has no memory of me: his face is turned away; he will never see it.
For the evil-doer is lifted up because of the purpose of his heart, and he whose mind is fixed on wealth is turned away from the Lord, saying evil against him.
And may your hearts be guided by the Lord into the love of God and quiet waiting for Christ.
Let the Lord be praised. I will give praise to the Lord with all my heart, among the upright, and in the meeting of the people.
For you see the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, how though he had wealth, he became poor on your account, so that through his need you might have wealth.
Do good, O Lord, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in heart.
But as for such as are turned out of the straight way, the Lord will take them away with the workers of evil. Let peace be on Israel.
For God is my witness, how my love goes out to you all in the loving mercies of Christ Jesus.
And the God of peace will be crushing Satan under your feet before long. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
Because of this my spirit is overcome; and my heart is full of fear.
The orders of the Lord are right, making glad the heart: the rule of the Lord is holy, giving light to the eyes.
The poor will have a feast of good things: those who make search for the Lord will give him praise: your heart will have life for ever.
Do not keep in mind my sins when I was young, or my wrongdoing: let your memory of me be full of mercy, O Lord, because of your righteousness.
The Lord is my strength and my breastplate, my heart had faith in him and I am helped; for this cause my heart is full of rapture, and I will give him praise in my song.
So that on the Gentiles might come the blessing of Abraham in Christ Jesus; in order that we through faith might have the Spirit which God had undertaken to give.
Be glad in the Lord with joy, you upright men; give cries of joy, all you whose hearts are true.
The Lord's purpose is eternal, the designs of his heart go on through all the generations of man.
Let those who are on my side give cries of joy; let them ever say, The Lord be praised, for he has pleasure in the peace of his servant.
And not only so, but we have joy in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we are now at peace with God.
Have faith in the Lord, and do good; be at rest in the land, and go after righteousness.
The law of his God is in his heart; he will never make a false step.
So will your delight be in the Lord, and he will give you your heart's desires.
Lord, you have put joy in my heart, more than they have when their grain and their wine are increased.
My delight is to do your pleasure, O my God; truly, your law is in my heart.
You have seen it; for your eyes are on sorrow and grief, to take it into your hand: the poor man puts his faith in you; you have been the helper of the child who has no father.
Give praise to the Lord, O my soul. O Lord my God, you are very great; you are robed with honour and power.
But, O Lord God, give me your help, because of your name; take me out of danger, because your mercy is good.
And that as you are full of every good thing, of faith, of the word, of knowledge, of a ready mind, and of love to us, so you may be full of this grace in the same way.
The Lord will send out the rod of your strength from Zion; be king over your haters.
O house of Aaron, have faith in the Lord: he is their help and their breastplate.
You worshippers of the Lord, have faith in the Lord: he is their help and their breastplate.
O Israel, have faith in the Lord: he is their help and their breastplate.
And may the God of peace himself make you holy in every way; and may your spirit and soul and body be free from all sin at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Be quick in answering me, O Lord, for the strength of my spirit is gone: let me see your face, so that I may not be like those who go down into the underworld.
Great is our Lord, and great his power; there is no limit to his wisdom.
But praise be to God who gives us strength to overcome through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Be lifted up, O Lord, in your strength; so will we make songs in praise of your power.
Do not be far from me, O Lord: O my strength, come quickly to my help.
Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Give ear to me, O Lord, and have mercy on me: Lord, be my helper.
May the Lord be praised, because he has made clear to me the wonder of his grace in a strong town.
Your mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens, and your strong purpose is as high as the clouds.
But the Lord is the saviour of the upright: he is their strength in the time of trouble.
The Lord will be his support on his bed of pain: by you will all his grief be turned to strength.
And Jesus was increasing in wisdom and in years, and in grace before God and men.
Grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Your righteousness has not been folded away in my heart; I have made clear your true word and your salvation; I have not kept secret your mercy or your faith from the great meeting.
I said to myself, The Lord is my heritage; and because of this I will have hope in him.
But as for me, let my prayer be made to you, O Lord, at a time when you are pleased; O God, give me an answer in your great mercy, for your salvation is certain.
And he, answering, said, Have love for the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind; and for your neighbour as for yourself.
Let the Lord be praised. O give praise to the Lord, for he is good: for his mercy is unchanging for ever.

For you have been acting with deceit in your hearts; for you sent me to the Lord your God, saying, Make prayer for us to the Lord our God, and give us word of everything he may say, and we will do it.
Let men give praise to the Lord for his mercy, and for the wonders which he does for the children of men!
If then God gave them, when they had faith in the Lord Jesus Christ, the same as he gave to us, who was I to go against God?
And the Lord your God is to be loved with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength.
And you are to have love for the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.
Certain for ever is the memory of his wonders: the Lord is full of pity and mercy.
Then I made my prayer to the Lord, saying, O Lord, take my soul out of trouble.
The Lord is full of grace and righteousness; truly, he is a God of mercy.
For great is his mercy to us, and his faith is unchanging for ever. Praise be to the Lord.
But we have faith that we will get salvation through the grace of the Lord Jesus in the same way as they.
Keep the form of those true words which you had from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
Let all worshippers of the Lord now say, that his mercy is unchanging for ever.
I am greatly troubled, O Lord, give me life in keeping with your word.
Let my voice come to you, in your mercy; O Lord, by your decisions give me life.
See how great is my love for your orders: give me life, O Lord, in keeping with your mercy.
Great is the number of your mercies, O Lord; give me life in keeping with your decisions.
Who through him have faith in God who took him up again from the dead into glory; so that your faith and hope might be in God.
Your righteousness is like the mountains of God; your judging is like the great deep; O Lord, you give life to man and beast.
Give the offerings of righteousness, and put your faith in the Lord.
But you, O Lord, have mercy on me, lifting me up, so that I may give them their punishment.
Keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for life eternal through the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Before the Lord, for he is come; he is come to be the judge of the earth; the earth will be judged in righteousness, and the peoples with unchanging faith.
Lord, where are your earlier mercies? where is the oath which you made to David in unchanging faith?
So you will have grace and a good name in the eyes of God and men.
For whoever gets me gets life, and grace from the Lord will come to him.
As a father has pity on his children, so the Lord has pity on his worshippers.
Let the wise give thought to these things, and see the mercies of the Lord.
For the Lord is upright; he is a lover of righteousness: the upright will see his face.
Let the doors of righteousness be open to me; I will go in and give praise to the Lord.
This is the door of the Lord's house; the workers of righteousness will go in through it.
But Christ as a son, over his house; whose house are we, if we keep our hearts fixed in the glad and certain hope till the end.
Give me teaching so that I may do your pleasure; for you are my God: let your good Spirit be my guide into the land of righteousness.
As for God, his way is completely good; the word of the Lord is tested; he is a breastplate for all those who put their faith in him.
He put his faith in the Lord; let the Lord be his saviour now: let the Lord be his saviour, because he had delight in him.
O my God, I have put my faith in you, let me not be shamed; let not my haters be glorying over me.
And the grace of our Lord was very great, with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
But I had faith in you, O Lord; I said, You are my God.
For the word of the Lord is upright, and all his works are certain.
The Lord will be the saviour of the souls of his servants, and no one who has faith in him will be put to shame.
And Stephen, while he was being stoned, made prayer to God, saying, Lord Jesus, take my spirit.
Put your life in the hands of the Lord; have faith in him and he will do it.
Happy is the man who has faith in the Lord, and does not give honour to the men of pride or to those who are turned away to deceit.
But you, O God, will send them down into the underworld; the cruel and the false will be cut off before half their days are ended; but I will have faith in you.
Because they had no faith in God, and no hope in his salvation.
In heaven let them give praise for your wonders, O Lord; and your unchanging faith among the saints.
We will be glad in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will put up our flags: may the Lord give you all your requests.
For they say that they are of the holy town, and put their faith in the God of Israel: the Lord of armies is his name.
He who gives attention to the law of right will get good; and whoever puts his faith in the Lord is happy.
I have given my love to the Lord, because he has given ear to the voice of my cry and my prayer.
Let your hearts be turned to me, so that you may have salvation, all the ends of the earth: for I am God, and there is no other.
The Lord has more love for the doors of Zion than for all the tents of Jacob.
We had loving talk together, and went to the house of God in company.
For this reason am I loved by the Father, because I give up my life so that I may take it again.
Jesus said to him in answer, If anyone has love for me, he will keep my words: and he will be dear to my Father; and we will come to him and make our living-place with him.
He who has no love for me does not keep my words; and the word which you are hearing is not my word but the Father's who sent me.
But he comes so that the world may see that I have love for the Father, and that I am doing as I am ordered by the Father. Get up, and let us go.
Even as the Father has given me his love, so I have given my love to you: be ever in my love.
The Father has love for the Son and has put all things into his hands.
For the Father has love for the Son and lets him see everything which he does: and he will let him see greater works than these so that you may be full of wonder.
Jesus said to them, If God was your Father you would have love for me, because it was from God I came and am here. I did not come of myself, but he sent me.
He who has more love for his father or mother than for me is not good enough for me; he who has more love for son or daughter than for me is not good enough for me.
See my servant, the man of my selection, my loved one in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my Spirit on him, and he will make my decision clear to the Gentiles.
Give honour to your father and your mother: and, Have love for your neighbour as for yourself.
For I am conscious that in a short time I will have to put off this tent of flesh, as our Lord Jesus Christ has made clear to me.
The Lord has let his holy arm be seen by the eyes of all nations; and all the ends of the earth will see the salvation of our God.
Happy are the people who have knowledge of the holy cry: the light of your face, O Lord, will be shining on their way.
Let all the earth send out a glad cry to the Lord; sounding with a loud voice, and praising him with songs of joy.
For our preaching is not about ourselves, but about Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants through Jesus.

In the day when God will be a judge of the secrets of men, as it says in the good news of which I am a preacher, through Jesus Christ.
I will give an offering of praise to you, and make my prayer in the name of the Lord.

I have said to the Lord, You are my God: give ear, O Lord, to the voice of my prayer.
O Lord, how great is the number of your works! in wisdom you have made them all; the earth is full of the things you have made.
Till the time when his word came true; he was tested by the word of the Lord.
The fear of the Lord is the best part of wisdom: all those who keep his laws are wise: his praise is eternal.
The Lord is God, and he has given us light; let the holy dance be ordered with branches, even up to the horns of the altar.
I have been true to your unchanging word; O Lord, do not put me to shame.
The upright will be glad in the Lord and have hope in him; and all the lovers of righteousness will give him glory.
In God will I give praise to his word; in God have I put my hope; I will have no fear of what flesh may do to me.
O Lord, our God, other lords than you have had rule over us; but in you only is our salvation, and no other name will we take on our lips.
Give worship to the Lord with joy; come before him with a song.
And going even farther than our hope, they first gave themselves to the Lord and to us after the purpose of God.
Give praise to the Lord, O my soul; let not all his blessings go from your memory.
Give praise to the Lord, all his works, in all places under his rule: give praise to the Lord, O my soul.
Then give no attention to the words of that prophet or that dreamer of dreams: for the Lord your God is testing you, to see if all the love of your heart and soul is given to him.
But now, says the Lord your Maker, O Jacob, and your life-giver, O Israel: have no fear, for I have taken up your cause; naming you by your name, I have made you mine.
